summary: Matched a large set of Peach turnip item functions by replacing placeholder stubs in itpeachturnip.c with typed implementations, adding the Peach turnip state table, defining Peach turnip attribute and item-var structs, and tightening itpeachturnip.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete item signatures. The work covers weighted random turnip type selection, turnip spawn/give initialization, owner cleanup, lifetime physics callbacks, animation/hitbox setup, collision responses, and item-var storage in the Item union.
what_changed: Replaced placeholder comments for many itpeachturnip.c functions with C implementations for functions from it_802BD32C through it_802BDA08.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F74A8 with five state entries referencing Peach turnip callbacks such as it_802BD6D4, it_802BD6DC, it_802BD6E0, it_802BD718, it_802BD7E0, it_802BD818, and it_802BD864.
Implemented it_802BD32C as a weighted random turnip type selector using itPeachTurnipAttributes, x4_length, x8[i].x0_odds, and HSD_Randi(sum).

Implemented it_802BD4AC as Peach_SpawnAndGiveTurnip-style logic that builds a SpawnItem, calls Item_80268B18, initializes turnip lifetime/type/damage/owner fields, attaches the item to Peach with Item_8026AB54, and caches scale values for turnip, Mr. Saturn, and Bob-omb cases.
Implemented turnip on-spawn/setup behavior in it_802BD628, including animation selection, item model update via it_80273670, it_8026BCF4 for newly spawned turnips, and Item_802694CC.
Implemented life timer update callbacks it_802BD6E0 and it_802BD7E0 that decrement item->xD44_lifeTimer and return true when it expires.
Implemented physics/update helpers that use ItemAttr fall speed values through it_80272860 and projectile velocity handling through it_80274658.
Implemented hitbox/damage setup in it_802BD748 by applying the selected turnip type, writing hit damage with it_80272460, and scaling the jobj with it_80274484.
Implemented collision response functions using helpers such as itColl_BounceOffVictim, itColl_BounceOffShield, it_80273030, it_8026DAA8, it_80276FC4, and Item_80268E5C.
Implemented owner-reference cleanup in it_802BD47C and it_802BDA08, including ftPe_SpecialLw_UnsetVeg when the turnip still has an owner.
Added itPeachTurnipAttributes and itPeachTurnip_ItemVars to itCommonItems.h.
Added itPeachTurnip_ItemVars peachturnip to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Updated itpeachturnip.h prototypes to use concrete return types and parameters such as s32, bool, void, Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, Fighter_Part, ItemKind, and f32 instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
smart_moves: Introduced a dedicated itPeachTurnipAttributes struct with a flexible x8[] odds/damage table, matching the weighted turnip-type logic without hard-coding all type data directly in code.

Separated persistent per-item state into itPeachTurnip_ItemVars at the known xDD4 item-var union offset, making later turnip functions access owner, type index, damage, flags, and scale through named fields rather than raw offsets.

Kept known-good informal names as comments, such as Peach_TurnipTypeDecide, Peach_SpawnAndGiveTurnip, ItemOnSpawn_Turnip, and ProjectilePhysics_TurnipVelocity, while preserving address-based symbol names for project consistency.
Converted headers in parallel with the implementation, which removed ambiguity from callback signatures and made the ItemStateTable entries type-check against concrete callback forms.

Handled special spawned-item cases by item kind: turnip and Mr. Saturn cache xDE0_scl while Bob-omb caches xDE8_scl, preserving item-specific scale behavior instead of treating all spawned items identically.
Cleared Peach's vegetable ownership state both when the turnip is removed from its owner path and when a referenced owner gobj matches in it_802BDA08, reducing stale-owner risk.
Used existing item and collision helpers rather than open-coding physics/collision behavior, which keeps the matched code aligned with surrounding item subsystem patterns.
decomp_lessons: For item decompilation, defining the item-specific xDD4 union member is often a prerequisite for readable and matchable function bodies; this PR added peachturnip before using fields throughout itpeachturnip.c.

Article special attributes can be modeled as a small header plus a trailing variable-length array when game data contains a count and repeated records; here x4_length controls the odds/damage entries at x8[].
Weighted random selection should mirror the original control flow closely, including temporary sentinel values and cumulative odds updates, because small loop-shape changes can affect matching.
Spawn functions often need to initialize the generic SpawnItem structure completely, including parent fields, facing direction, damage, velocity, flags, and x40, before calling Item_80268B18.
When matching item callbacks, update prototypes early from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to bool/void/concrete pointer types so state tables and call sites reveal mismatches.
Collision callback functions may be simple wrappers around shared helpers; matching progress can come from recognizing and naming those wrappers rather than overcomplicating them.
Owner or parent gobj references need explicit cleanup paths in item code; decomp work should look for comparisons against ref_gobj and calls into character-specific unset functions.
Turnip-related logic crosses item and fighter systems: this PR required ftPe_SpecialLw_UnsetVeg from Peach special-lw code as well as item subsystem helpers.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based function names such as it_802BD32C and it_802BD4AC in exported symbols.
Used item-specific struct names in the project style: itPeachTurnipAttributes and itPeachTurnip_ItemVars.
Named item-var union member peachturnip in lowercase to match nearby item-var union members such as mato, nokonoko, octarock, and pikachujoltground.
Preserved field-offset style names for unresolved fields, including x0_lifetime, x4_length, x8, x0_odds, x4_damage, xDD4, xDD8, xDDC_damage, xDE0_scl, xDE4_owner, and xDE8_scl.
Used comments to document semantic guesses without renaming symbols globally, e.g. xDD8 is noted as turnip type index, xDE0_scl as scale for Mr. Saturn/Turnip, and xDE8_scl as scale for Bob-omb.
Used informal function comments for likely semantic names: Peach_TurnipTypeDecide, Peach_SpawnAndGiveTurnip, ItemOnSpawn_Turnip, and ProjectilePhysics_TurnipVelocity.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Modeled it_802BD32C with explicit locals i, rand, sum, tmp, cum_odds, and ret, including tmp = -1 and ret == tmp loop termination, suggesting local/control-flow shaping for a match.
Used HSD_Randi(sum) after summing all odds, then compared the random value against cumulative odds to select the first matching turnip type.
Used item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes as the source for Peach turnip lifetime, odds, and damage data.
Initialized SpawnItem fields explicitly and in a concrete order before calling Item_80268B18, a common matching tactic for spawn routines.
Used chained zero initialization for spawn.vel.x, spawn.vel.y, and spawn.vel.z as 0.0F, matching common decomp style for vector zeroing.
Used concrete animation update flags such as ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and ITEM_DROP_UPDATE in Item_80268E5C calls.
Kept simple callbacks as direct returns or helper calls, such as it_802BD6D4 returning false and it_802BD6DC returning void with an explicit return.
For collision bitmask logic in it_802BD864, introduced first_check = 0xE and second_check = 0x3 locals and tested the result of it_8026DAA8 before calling it_80276FC4 or returning true.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.context: Reviewer reacted positively to the addition of itPeachTurnipAttributes with lifetime, length, odds, and damage fields.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further document itPeachTurnipAttributes fields x0_lifetime, x4_length, x0_odds, and x4_damage.
Find follow-up work on it_802BD864 where the TODO says to give descriptive names to variables first_check and second_check.
Search for later PRs that rename it_802BD32C or it_802BD4AC to semantic Peach turnip names.
Check whether it_802BDB94 was intentionally removed from itpeachturnip.h or handled in another PR.
Compare other item spawn-and-give routines for reusable SpawnItem initialization patterns.
